basics.name: Ehsan Barkhordar
basics.label: NLP & Computational Social Science Researcher
basics.summary: M.Sc. in Computer Science from Koç University (3.8/4.0 GPA), researching NLP, computational social science, bias detection, and agentic AI. Six-plus years of prior industry experience as an ML and software engineer.

work.name: Koç University
work.position: Researcher in NLP & Computational Social Science
highlights: Led a multimodal hate speech detection project integrating textual and visual modalities.
Authored three first-author papers in computational social science.
Reviewed 100+ papers for ARR, EMNLP, ICLR, and related venues.
Produced 65+ weekly progress reports for collaborative research tracking.
Trained and fine-tuned transformer-based models (BERT, RoBERTa, GPT) for NER and related tasks.
Supervised two research interns, providing mentorship and task delegation.

work.name: Pishro Net Energy
highlights: Designed and deployed end-to-end ML pipelines for real-time energy monitoring across 1,000+ sensor streams (10 buildings), reducing forecasting error by 21%.
Created data-driven dashboards for 150+ KPIs to support facility managers in decision-making.

work.name: Sokan Platform
highlights: Built predictive models for customer analytics, including churn prediction (82% accuracy) and cohort-based sales insights.
Optimized large-scale data pipelines (1B+ records) using TensorFlow, Kubeflow, and Mini-batch K-means.
Applied FP-Growth to optimize product bundling strategy, improving cross-sell opportunities and data-driven merchandising decisions.

work.name: Sokan Platform
highlights: Implemented asynchronous task management in Django with Celery, reducing computational overhead by 40%.
Accelerated analytics retrieval speed 5x via ClickHouse migration.
Delivered 50+ GraphQL APIs using Django Graphene, reducing frontend data-fetching overhead by 35% and consolidating multiple REST endpoints.
Architected and deployed automated CI/CD pipelines with Docker and GitLab, cutting deployment time by 60% and enabling 3x more frequent releases.

work.name: Bale Messenger
highlights: Contributed to the development of a cross-platform messaging application with 8M+ users.
Created a Python-based bot SDK adopted by 200+ developers, powering 500+ bots and broadening accessibility for both developers and non-developers.
Participated in migration to Golang microservices, achieving >7x performance gains.
Implemented monitoring pipelines with Prometheus and Grafana to track 20+ key system metrics.
Ranked #1 university in Turkey (QS 2026).
Advisors: Asst. Prof. Gözde Gül Şahin (primary), Assoc. Prof. Erdem Yörük (co-advisor).
Ranked #3 university in Iran (QS 2026).
Ranked #3 university in Iran (QS 2026).

awards.title: 2nd Rank — Hate Speech Detection in Turkish and Arabic Tweets (HSD-2Lang)
Invited Participant — Lisbon Machine Learning School (LxMLS 2023)
One of 5 Selected Teams — Fusion Startup Event

publications.name: Are Non-English Papers Reviewed Fairly? Language-of-Study Bias in NLP Peer Reviews
publications.summary: Ehsan Barkhordar, Abdulfattah Safa, Verena Blaschke, Erika Lombart, Marie-Catherine de Marneffe, Gözde Gül Şahin.

publications.name: Assessing the Predictive Power of Social Media Data-Fed Large Language Models on Vote Preference
publications.publisher: Companion Publication of the 16th ACM Web Science Conference, Stuttgart, Germany

publications.name: Why the Unexpected? Dissecting the Political and Economic Bias in Persian Small and Large Language Models
publications.publisher: Proceedings of the 3rd Annual Meeting of SIGUL @ LREC-COLING, Torino, Italy
publications.summary: Ehsan Barkhordar, Surendrabikram Thapa, Ashwarya Maratha, Usman Naseem.

publications.name: Team Curie at HSD-2Lang 2024: Hate Speech Detection in Turkish and Arabic Tweets using BERT-based Models
publications.publisher: Proceedings of the 7th Workshop on CASE, St. Julians, Malta
publications.summary: Ehsan Barkhordar, Işık Topçu, Ali Hürriyetoğlu.

publications.name: Which Side Are You On? Investigating Politico-Economic Bias in Nepali Language Models
publications.summary: Surendrabikram Thapa, Kritesh Rauniyar, Ehsan Barkhordar, Hariram Veeramani, Usman Naseem.

publications.name: Clustering of Bank Customers using LSTM-based Encoder-Decoder and Dynamic Time Warping
publications.summary: Ehsan Barkhordar, Mohammad Hassan Shirali-Shahreza, Hamid Reza Sadeghi.

projects.name: LLM Collusion and Identifiability in Code Generation
projects.summary: Investigates whether large language models can recognize or attribute code authored by themselves or other LLMs.
highlights: Implemented three attribution tasks: Self-Recognition, Target Identification, and Full Attribution.
Evaluated across MBPP, HumanEval, and DS-1000 with code generated by GPT-5, Claude, Gemini, Grok, and DeepSeek.
Fine-tuned lightweight judge models to identify model-specific code signatures.

projects.name: Multimodal Framework for Detecting Implicit Hate Speech on Social Media
projects.summary: Hybrid framework combining text encoders (BERT, LLaMA) with vision backbones (Swin Transformer, ViT) to detect subtle hate speech.
highlights: Improved robustness via focal loss, progressive unfreezing, and adversarial training.
Demonstrated superior performance on multimodal hate speech datasets, addressing explainability and ethical implications.

projects.name: Named Entity Recognition in Turkish Tweets (Yildirim Platform)
projects.summary: Developed an annotation platform (Yildirim) for labeling named entities in Turkish tweets.
highlights: Annotated 5,000 tweets from a 700M+ tweet dataset to improve NER performance on low-resource, informal language.

projects.name: Procedural Text Understanding via Python Code Representation
projects.summary: Explored Python code representation as an intermediate reasoning layer for LLMs.
highlights: Code-based prompting improved accuracy from 68.29% to 86.76%.

projects.name: Intent Prediction and Slot Filling with Alexa MASSIVE Dataset
projects.summary: Multilingual intent prediction and slot filling on the Alexa MASSIVE dataset.
highlights: Participated in the Massively Multilingual NLU 2022 shared task.
Trained multilingual intent and slot-filling models in PyTorch.

projects.name: Shilling Attack Detection in Recommender Systems
projects.summary: Investigated detection of profile injection (shilling) attacks in recommender systems.
highlights: Extracted RDMA, WDA, and TF-IDF features; evaluated multiple classifiers (KNN, Random Forest, SVM).

languages.fluency: Fluent — TOEFL iBT 103/120 (R:26, L:28, S:25, W:24), Dec 2024
